name: Cry Hindutva How Rhetoric Trumps Intellect

author: Vishwa Adluri, Joydeep Bagchee

author: Vishwa Adluri, Joydeep Bagchee

summary: Study of German orientalism and the ideological lenses shaping Indological interpretation.

name: The Origins of Racism in the Humanities

author: Vishwa Adluri, Joydeep Bagchee
summary: Programmatic theses outlining a critique of Indology and an alternative scholarly orientation.

name: Hindu Dharma

summary: Scholarly work on Indian thought and civilization with relevance to the themes signaled by its title.

author: Unknown

summary: Essay by S. Radhakrishnan presenting Hindu dharma as a living, adaptive, and philosophically grounded religious tradition.

summary: Traditional practical compendium on useful arts, household knowledge, and applied sciences.

summary: Another edition of the Mimamsa Sutras presenting the core framework of Vedic hermeneutics and duty.

name: Logic of Rig Vedic Geography

name: Dravidian Connections to Rig Veda and Harappa

author: Manogna Sastry, Megh Kalyanasundaram

summary: Traces the hydrological and historical evolution of the Sarasvati River, exploring its shift in Vedic texts and its modern identification with ancient river systems.

name: Sarasvatī in the Mahābhārata - A Study
author: Sastry and Kalyanasundaram
summary: Analyzes references to the Sarasvati River in the Mahabharata, mapping its geographical and mythological significance across epic literature.

name: The Sarasvati River Issues And Debates
author: Michel Danino
summary: A comprehensive overview of academic debates surrounding the Sarasvati River, blending geology, archaeology, and Vedic textual evidence.

name: Sarasvati Palaeochannel Groundwater Remote Sensing
author: Kar and Shukla

name: On The Preliminary Periodization Of The Harappan Civilization
author: Igor A. Tonoyan-Belyayev
summary: Proposes a revised chronological framework for the Harappan civilization, aligning archaeological phases with early Vedic cultural developments.

name: Vedic Roots Of Early Tamil Culture
author: Michel Danino
summary: Explores linguistic and cultural connections between early Vedic traditions and ancient Tamil Sangam literature, challenging rigid linguistic divides.

name: The Indo Aryans Of Ancient South Asia
author: Michael Witzel
summary: A linguistic and archaeological synthesis on Indo-Aryan migrations, examining language shift, material culture, and ethnic interactions in ancient South Asia.

summary: Examines archaeological evidence of horses in ancient India, critically analyzing claims used in the Aryan migration versus indigenous origin debate.

name: The Coming of Aryans to Iran and India

summary: A scholarly analysis of Indo-Iranian migrations into Iran and India, exploring linguistic, religious, and archaeological markers of early Aryan presence.

name: Rig Vedic History
author: Michael Witzel
summary: Reconstructs early Indian history through Rigvedic linguistic parameters, mapping tribal movements, geographical shifts, and cultural developments.

name: Rama's Realm
author: Michael Witzel
summary: Investigates the geographical and historical context of Rama's kingdom in the Ramayana, analyzing textual layers and regional cultural influences.

name: On the Battle of Ten Kings
author: Ranier Stuhrmann
summary: A detailed philological and historical analysis of the Dasharajna battle in the Rig Veda, examining tribal alliances and geopolitical implications.

name: On Talageri
author: Michael Witzel
summary: Critical review of Shrikant Talageri's Out of India theories, contrasting traditional Vedic chronology with mainstream Indo-European migration models.

name: Non Aryan Names In Rig Veda
author: Michael Witzel
summary: Linguistic study identifying non-Indo-Aryan personal and place names in the Rig Veda, exploring substrate influences and cultural exchanges.

name: New Perspectives On Our Cultural Pasts
author: Michel Danino
summary: Advocates for interdisciplinary approaches to Indian history, integrating archaeology, genetics, and textual studies to revise cultural narratives.

name: Vedic Genetics Cow Omni Form Decipherment
author: Chandra Prakash Dvivedi

name: The Prehistoric Peopling Of Southeast Asia

summary: Genomic analysis tracing ancient human migrations into Southeast Asia, offering insights into population continuity and dispersal patterns.

name: The Himalayas As A Prehistoric Corridor
author: George van Driem
summary: Examines the Himalayan region as a vital prehistoric migration route, connecting linguistic and genetic evidence of ancient population movements.

name: The Formation of Human Populations in South and Central Asia

summary: Landmark ancient DNA study mapping population formations in South and Central Asia, highlighting Steppe pastoralist migrations and local admixture events.

name: The Eastern Himalayan Corridor In Prehistory
author: George van Driem
summary: Linguistic and archaeological analysis of the Eastern Himalayas as a prehistoric corridor, tracing Austroasiatic and Sino-Tibetan dispersals.

name: Reconstructing The Population History Of the Gond
author: Chaubey et al.
summary: Genomic study reconstructing the demographic history of the Gond tribe, revealing ancient Indian population structure and migration patterns.

name: Prehistoric Skeletal Record of Man in South Asia
author: Kenneth AR Kennedy
summary: Comprehensive bioarchaeological review of prehistoric skeletal remains in South Asia, assessing morphological continuity and population changes.

name: Prehistoric Human Colonisation Of India
author: VN Misra
summary: Archaeological synthesis on early human settlement in India, tracing Paleolithic to Neolithic cultural transitions and environmental adaptations.

name: Origin Of Rice
author: Civan et al.
summary: Genomic analysis tracing the domestication and spread of rice in Asia, highlighting multiple independent origins and ancient agricultural networks.

name: On Peopling of India Ancient Genetics Perspectives
author: Thangaraj and Rai
summary: Reviews ancient and modern genetic data to reconstruct India's demographic history, evaluating migration waves and indigenous continuity.

name: Linguistic Population Prehistory Of The Greater Himalaya Region
author: George van Driem
summary: Correlates linguistic diversity with population history in the Greater Himalayas, mapping ancient language families and migratory routes.

name: Haplogroup R1A
author: Maciamo
summary: Comprehensive overview of Haplogroup R1a distribution, tracing its origins, Bronze Age expansions, and relevance to Indo-European migrations.

name: Glimpses Of The Ethnolinguistic Prehistory of Northeastern India
author: George van Driem
summary: Explores the ethnolinguistic layers of Northeast India, analyzing Tibeto-Burman, Austroasiatic, and Indo-Aryan interactions in prehistory.

name: Genetics And The Aryan Issue
author: Michel Danino
summary: Critical evaluation of genetic evidence in the Aryan debate, addressing misinterpretations and emphasizing continuity in South Asian populations.

name: Genetics And AIT, Look Again
author: Koenraad Elst
summary: Re-examines genetic claims supporting the Aryan Invasion Theory, highlighting methodological flaws and alternative interpretations of population data.

name: Genetic And Linguistic Non Correspondence
author: Srivastava et al.
summary: Study demonstrating decoupling of genetic ancestry and language spread in South Asia, challenging strict migration-based linguistic models.

name: Genes, Peoples and Languages
author: LL Cavalli-Sforza
summary: Seminal work exploring correlations between genetic variation, human populations, and linguistic families across global and regional scales.

name: Worked Bone Industry
author: Chattopadhyay and Bandopadhyay
summary: Archaeological study of bone tool industries in the Ganga Valley, tracing technological continuity from Mesolithic to early historic periods.

name: The Sanauli Chariot And Its Archaeological and Historical Context
author: Giacomo Benedetti
summary: Analyzes the Sanauli chariot burials, evaluating their technological features and implications for Bronze Age warfare and Vedic chronology.

name: The Harappan Heritage And The Aryan Problem
author: Michel Danino
summary: Examines cultural and archaeological continuity between the Indus Valley Civilization and early Vedic culture, addressing the Aryan migration narrative.

name: The Epistemological Event Horizon In Archaeology
author: George van Driem
summary: Philosophical critique of archaeological interpretation, addressing methodological limits in reconstructing prehistoric language and population movements.

name: Sibri and the South Cemetery of Mehrgarh
author: Marielle Santoni
summary: Archaeological report on the Sibri burial ground near Mehrgarh, analyzing funerary practices and cultural links to early South Asian settlements.

name: Sanauli a Late Harappan Burial Site
author: Sharma et al.
summary: Detailed excavation report on Sanauli burials, highlighting Late Harappan material culture, chariot finds, and regional trade connections.

name: Origin Of Early Harappan Cultures In The Sarasvati Valley
author: KN Dikshit
summary: Archaeological synthesis tracing the emergence of Early Harappan cultures in the Ghaggar-Hakra basin, supported by radiometric dating.

name: Indus Valley Electron Microscopy And Crystallography
author: Chandra Prakash Trivedi
summary: Scientific analysis of Indus Valley artifacts using electron microscopy, revealing advanced metallurgical and craftsmanship techniques.

name: Vishnu In Rigveda
author: Dmitri Semenov
summary: Philological study of Vishnu's early presence in the Rig Veda, tracing the evolution of solar and cosmic attributes in Vedic hymns.

name: True Meaning Of Ashva
author: Aleksandr Semenenko
summary: Etymological and contextual analysis of 'Ashva' in the Rig Veda, exploring symbolic, ritual, and literal interpretations of the term.

name: The Indo European Cloudland
author: Michel Danino
summary: Explores mythological and linguistic parallels in Indo-European storm and cloud deities, reconstructing shared prehistoric cosmological concepts.

name: The Debate Between Talageri and Hoch
author: Koenraad Elst
summary: Critical overview of the academic exchange between Shrikant Talageri and Hans Heinrich Hock on Vedic chronology and Indo-European origins.

name: The A of ABC of Indian chronology: Dimensions of the Aryan problem revisited in 2017
author: Sastry and Kalyanasundaram
summary: Revisits foundational chronology debates in Indian history, integrating textual, archaeological, and astronomical data to reassess Aryan timelines.

name: Short Notes On Paninian Sanskrit Versus Mature Harappan
author: Igor A. Tonoyan-Belyayev
summary: Comparative linguistic analysis contrasting Paninian Sanskrit structures with hypothesized Mature Harappan language features.

name: Rigvedic And Written Mature Harappan 2
author: Igor A. Tonoyan-Belyayev
summary: Continues the exploration of linguistic parallels between Rigvedic Sanskrit and the undeciphered Indus script, proposing structural correlations.

name: Rigvedic And Written Mature Harappan 1
author: Igor A. Tonoyan-Belyayev
summary: Initial analysis proposing linguistic continuity between Rigvedic vocabulary and Indus Valley inscriptions, challenging invasion narratives.

name: On the Meaning of Hymns to Maruts in the Rigveda
author: Dmitri Semenov
summary: Philological examination of Marut hymns in the Rig Veda, exploring storm mythology, ritual significance, and Indo-Iranian parallels.

name: Munda Languages Are Father Tongues But Japanese and Korean Are Not
author: George van Driem
summary: Linguistic study on Austroasiatic Munda languages as substrates in South Asia, contrasting with proposed East Asian language family connections.

name: Methodological Issues In The Indo European Debate
author: Michel Danino
summary: Critical assessment of methodologies used in Indo-European studies, advocating for interdisciplinary rigor and bias reduction in historical linguistics.

name: Iranians Vs Vedic Aryans
author: Koenraad Elst
summary: Explores historical and linguistic divergences between Vedic Aryans and ancient Iranians, analyzing religious, cultural, and geopolitical splits.

name: Indo Uralic Indo Anatolian Indo Tocharian
author: Michael Peyrot
summary: Comparative linguistic analysis of early Indo-European branches, examining Anatolian, Tocharian, and proposed Uralic connections.

name: Images Of Solar Bulls
author: Aleksandr Semenenko
summary: Iconographic study of solar bull motifs across Early Harappan, Vedic, and Avestan traditions, tracing shared Indo-Iranian religious symbolism.

name: Harappan Discontinuity 4500 Ybp
author: Pokharia et al.
summary: Paleobotanical and archaeological evidence of cultural discontinuities around 4500 YBP, linking climate shifts to Harappan urban decline.

name: In Search Of The Oldest Common Indo European Urheimat
author: Igor A. Tonoyan-Belyayev
summary: Linguistic reconstruction proposing Dravidian loanwords and structural evidence for locating the earliest Indo-European homeland in South Asia.

name: Antecedents Of Rig Vedic Religion
author: Michael Witzel
summary: Traces Central Asian and Hindukush cultural influences on early Vedic religious practices, mapping ritual and mythological developments.

name: Excavating Vedic Harappans
author: Koenraad Elst
summary: Synthesizes archaeological findings demonstrating cultural continuity between Indus Valley settlements and early Vedic material culture.

name: Coexistence of Culture and Civilization ISC and OCP
author: Sontakke and Sontakke
summary: Analyzes the coexistence and interaction between Indus Valley Civilization and Ganga Plain Chalcolithic cultures, highlighting trade and cultural exchange.

name: Animal Subsistence In Ganga Valley
author: PP Joglekar
summary: Zooarchaeological study of animal husbandry and hunting practices in the Ganga Valley, tracing subsistence strategies from prehistoric to early historic periods.

name: Eurasian Spreadzone And IE Dispersal
author: Johanna Nichols
summary: Linguistic typology study on Eurasian language spread zones, modeling mechanisms behind Indo-European linguistic expansion across continents.

name: Ancient Cattle Genomics
author: Verdugo et al.
summary: Genomic analysis of ancient cattle domestication in South Asia, revealing independent taurine and indicine lineages and their historical dispersal.

name: Epicenter of the Indo-European Linguistic Spread
author: Johanna Nichols
summary: Identifies potential linguistic epicenters for Indo-European expansion, evaluating geographic, demographic, and typological factors in language dispersal.

name: Chronology And Culture History In The Indus Valley
author: Mark Manuel
summary: Comprehensive chronological framework for Indus Valley cultural phases, integrating stratigraphy, artifact typology, and regional settlement patterns.

name: AIT And Linguistics
author: Koenraad Elst
summary: Critical evaluation of linguistic arguments used to support the Aryan Invasion Theory, highlighting methodological assumptions and alternative models.

name: Discovering The Sarasvati River
author: Michel Danino
summary: Historical overview of Sarasvati River research from 19th-century explorations to modern hydrological and archaeological discoveries.

name: Chera Chola Pandya Using Archaeological Evidence to Identify the Tamil Kingdoms of Early Historic South India
author: Shinu Abraham
summary: Archaeological synthesis identifying material culture, trade networks, and political structures of early Chera, Chola, and Pandya kingdoms.

name: Absence Of Sword From Rig Veda
author: Aleksandr Semenenko
summary: Philological and archaeological analysis of the missing sword terminology in the Rig Veda, discussing implications for Indo-Aryan chronology.

name: Demilitarizing The Rigveda
author: Michel Danino
summary: Re-evaluates Vedic texts to contextualize references to horses, chariots, and warfare, challenging militarized interpretations of early Aryan culture.

name: Autochthonous Aryans
author: Michael Witzel
summary: Academic critique of indigenous Aryan theories, contrasting textual and linguistic evidence with mainstream Indo-European migration models.

name: A Timeline Of Ayodhya
author: Nicole Elfi and Michel Danino
summary: Historical and archaeological timeline of Ayodhya, tracing settlement layers, textual references, and cultural continuity from ancient to medieval periods.

name: Decipherment Of Indus Valley Vedic Seals
author: Chandra Prakash Trivedi
summary: Proposes Vedic linguistic correlations for Indus Valley seal inscriptions, integrating symbolic analysis with early Sanskrit vocabulary.

name: Asura And Its Derivatives In Rigveda
author: Dmitri Semenov
summary: Philological study of 'Asura' and related terms in the Rig Veda, tracing semantic shifts from divine epithets to later mythological antagonists.

name: A Prehistoric Thoroughfare Between The Ganges and the Himalaya
author: George van Driem
summary: Linguistic and archaeological mapping of ancient trade and migration routes connecting the Ganga basin to Himalayan foothills.

name: From Sarasvati To Ganga
author: Michel Danino
summary: Traces the cultural and geographical shift from Sarasvati-centered Vedic civilization to Ganga-dominated historical India, analyzing hydrological changes.

name: Decipherment Of Indus Valley Seals
author: Srini Kalyanaraman
summary: Proposes a rebus-based decipherment model for Indus script, linking iconography to early Dravidian and Sanskrit linguistic structures.

name: Astronomical Chronology Of Vedic Literature
author: Koenraad Elst
summary: Uses archaeoastronomy to date Vedic texts based on celestial references, proposing earlier chronologies for Rigvedic and post-Vedic compositions.

name: A Note On PIE And Nuclear Nostratic
author: Igor A. Tonoyan-Belyayev
summary: Preliminary linguistic analysis exploring potential genetic links between Proto-Indo-European and the hypothetical Nuclear Nostratic macrofamily.

name: Five Waves Of Indo European Expansion
author: Igor A. Tonoyan-Belyayev
summary: Proposes a five-wave migration model for Indo-European linguistic and cultural dispersal, integrating archaeological and genetic timelines.

name: Converging Views On Asian Prehistory
author: George van Driem
summary: Synthesizes linguistic, genetic, and archaeological perspectives on Asian prehistory, highlighting convergent evidence for population movements.

name: Aryans And The Indus Civilization
author: Michel Danino
summary: Examines archaeological and textual evidence for cultural continuity between Indus Valley settlements and early Vedic societies.

name: Females In Rig Veda
author: Michael Witzel
summary: Philological study of female figures, goddesses, and social roles in the Rig Veda, analyzing gender representation in early Vedic society.

name: Continuity Of European Languages
author: Anton Perdih
summary: Linguistic analysis tracing substrate continuity in European languages, evaluating pre-Indo-European influences on modern linguistic structures.

name: Archaeological and Anthropological Studies on the Harappan Cemetery of Rakhigarhi
author: Shinde et a.
summary: Comprehensive bioarchaeological report on Rakhigarhi burials, revealing Harappan health, diet, and population structure through skeletal analysis.

name: A Dravido Harappan Connection The Issue
author: Michel Danino
summary: Methodological critique of proposed Dravidian-Indus links, evaluating linguistic, archaeological, and epigraphic evidence for cultural continuity.

name: Indian Historical Writing
author: Michael Witzel
summary: Analyzes Indian historical writing traditions, focusing on Vaṃśāvalīs and their relationship to Vedic oral transmission and chronicle composition.

name: Fabricating Evidence In Support Of The AIT
author: Michel Danino
summary: Critical examination of archaeological and linguistic claims used to support the Aryan Invasion Theory, addressing methodological biases.

name: Complex Genetic Origin of Indian Populations and Its Implications
author: Tamang et al.
summary: Genomic study revealing complex admixture patterns in Indian populations, highlighting ancient migrations, endogamy, and regional genetic diversity.

name: Archaeobotany Of Ganga Plain 2500 BC
author: Pokharia et al.
summary: Paleobotanical analysis of crop remains in the Ganga Plain around 2500 BC, reconstructing early agricultural practices and environmental adaptations.

name: Scratching my head! — Criticism of Narsimhan VM et al 2019
author: Ashish Kulkarni
summary: Independent critique of Narasimhan et al. 2019 ancient DNA study, addressing sampling limitations and alternative interpretations of Steppe ancestry.

name: Chronology Of Kings Puranic Kings and Rigvedic Rshis
author: Giacomo Benedetti
summary: Cross-references Puranic king lists and Rigvedic rishi genealogies with archaeological phases of the Sindhu-Sarasvati civilization.

name: Aryas, Dasas and Dasyus
author: Shrikant Talageri
summary: Analyzes Rigvedic terminology for Aryas, Dasas, and Dasyus, reinterpreting socio-cultural conflicts as intra-tribal rather than ethnic invasions.

name: Aryan Story vs True Aryan History
author: Shrikant Talageri
summary: Contrasts mainstream Aryan migration narratives with textual and archaeological evidence supporting indigenous Vedic cultural development in India.

name: Varsagira Battle
author: Shrikant Talageri
summary: Historical and geographical analysis of the Varsagira battle in the Rig Veda, mapping tribal movements and early Vedic geopolitical shifts.

name: Rakhigarhi and After
author: Shrikant Talageri
summary: Critical response to Rakhigarhi ancient DNA findings, evaluating implications for Aryan migration theories and indigenous population continuity.

name: Out of India Case in Full
author: Shrikant Talageri
summary: Comprehensive synthesis of linguistic, archaeological, and textual arguments supporting the Out of India theory for Indo-European origins.

name: The Indo European Language Family
author: Thomas Olander
summary: Authoritative overview of Indo-European linguistic families, tracing phonological evolution, grammatical structures, and historical dispersal patterns.
